text: Fill in the blanks about the anatomy of the eyeball (AN41.1-3).
questions: The eyeball has three concentric coats: the outer *fibrous/fibrous coat* coat, the middle *vascular/uveal* coat, and the inner *neural/retinal* coat.
The cornea accounts for approximately *two-thirds/2/3* of the total refractive power of the eye and is supplied by the *ophthalmic/CN V1* division of the trigeminal nerve via long ciliary nerves.
The *ciliary body/ciliary* secretes aqueous humour and contains the ciliary muscle for *accommodation/focusing*, which is innervated by *parasympathetic/CN III* fibres via the ciliary ganglion.
The *fovea centralis/fovea* is the region of highest visual acuity containing only *cones/cone cells*, while the *optic disc/blind spot* is where the optic nerve exits and contains no photoreceptors.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the topic and try again.
